items.title: Worst-case modelling assumes rationing at 10 days' worth of diesel supply

children.content: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

items.title: Fuel purchasing powers could be used for nearly anything facing supply disruption

children.content: Broad powers to support companies to buy more fuel could be used on any good facing supply chain disruption — with concern that future governments could abuse the broad power.

image.altText: Fuel tanker

items.title: Insiders profit as pressure points build on global economy

children.content: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

image.altText: A satellite view of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Kharg Island if Iran refuses deal to end war

children.content: It comes as thousands more American troops arrive in the Middle East, with the US president yet to decide on the next course of action.

articles.title: Albanese wants Trump to recognise damage Iran war has caused

children.content: Anthony Albanese wants "more certainty" from the United States on its war objectives in Iran and says the administration must be clear if it is still pursuing regime change.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump standing in a doorway aboard Air Force One.

items.title: China and Iran's oil corridor signals a new global divide

children.content: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

image.altText: Xi Jinping

items.title: Israel passes death penalty for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks

children.content: The law, long-sought by Israel's far-right politicians, makes death by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu looking stern at a press conference.

items.title: Police killer Dezi Freeman may be dead but many questions still remain

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

gallery.altText: An image showing a middle-aged man staring down the barrel of the camera.

thumbnail.altText: An image showing a middle-aged man staring down the barrel of the camera.

items.title: Police continue hunt for anyone who assisted fugitive Dezi Freeman

children.content: Police say they will come down hard on anyone who helped Dezi Freeman, who was shot dead by police yesterday after a massive seven-month manhunt.

image.altText: Man holding phone horizontally while wearing body camera on a property that looks like a farm with mountain in the background

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry returning from France. Within minutes of trying to board he found out his identity had been stolen.

image.altText: A man dressed in large ears and a Pinoccio nose with a police hat.

items.title: Massive Matildas hype hasn't translated to A-League Women fandom

children.content: Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.

image.altText: An electronic sign with a blue background, with the number 1857 on it.

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill, and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

gallery.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

thumbnail.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

children.content: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

image.altText: A red-haired woman in a light-coloured blazer stands in a corridor.

items.title: 50 per cent of Australians delayed health care last year, mainly due to cost

children.content: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

image.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

items.title: As war rages in the Middle East, this bittersweet Iraqi film brings hope

children.content: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

gallery.altText: People in a street holding up images and cut outs of Saddam Hussein.

thumbnail.altText: People in a street holding up images and cut outs of Saddam Hussein.

items.title: Will you fly high or fail to launch on our moon quiz?

children.content: As NASA gets ready to launch humans around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, buckle up and let's see if your space knowledge survives our quiz.

image.altText: Image of half a Moon against a black background.

items.title: How to tell if your home is underinsured

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

image.altText: Grandfather and grandson walking to a yellow weatherboard home across a green lawn

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

items.title: These states have introduced free public transport amid fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel prices continue to rise some states have moved to make public transport free, while others say that fares are already low enough. Find out where.

image.altText: Train crosses over bridge above water with view of Brisbane city in the background

items.title: Safety officers to patrol Hobart CBD to curb anti-social behaviour

children.content: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will soon be patrolling "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb anti-social behaviour and theft.

image.altText: An empty shopping mall strip.

items.title: What led to Dezi Freeman's shootout with police that killed two

children.content: Dezi Freeman fatally shot two officers and injured a third before he vanished into remote bushland. Here is what we know about the events leading up to the fatal shooting — and the months-long manhunt that followed.

articles.title: Victorian premier says 'an evil man is dead' after Dezi Freeman killed — as it happened

children.content: The 56-year-old had been a fugitive since a shooting at his property that killed two police officers.

image.altText: Man holding phone horizontally while wearing body camera on a property that looks like a farm with mountain in the background

articles.title: Dezi Freeman death 'a relief' for slain police officer's best mate

children.content: The best friend of slain Porepunkah detective Neal Thompson says the police shooting of fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman gives "closure", though it will not bring back his mate.

image.altText: A man with short white/grey hair in a colourful shirt looking away

articles.title: The volatile, 'unemployable' man who became a notorious police killer

children.content: Before Dezi Freeman's death brought an end to a long manhunt, his friends say he was a troubled and divisive figure who was drawn to the anti-authority "sovereign citizen" movement.

children.content: Tasked with training AI systems to perform jobs traditionally done by humans, former PwC employee Donald King says he faced a moral dilemma.

items.title: Myanmar's military chief makes move to become next president

children.content: Myanmar's military chief has stepped down to seek the presidency in the general's latest efforts to maintain power behind the facade of a civilian administration.

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent

children.content: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

items.title: Banned Xerri reveals Essendon abuse leading to blood-wiping incident

children.content: Kangaroos ruckman Tristan Xerri gives evidence at the AFL tribunal as he accepts a suspension for rubbing blood from his nose on the face of Essendon captain Andrew McGrath.

items.title: Economists warn fuel price cut likely to come with 'sting in the tail'

children.content: Australians will pay about 26.3 cents less on every litre of fuel they buy for three months, but some economists warn it could ultimately leave Australians worse off.

articles.title: Australia's four-step plan to survive fuel crisis

children.content: Fuel could be rationed and funnelled towards critical sectors such as emergency services under worst-case-scenario planning for shortages caused by the Iran war.

image.altText: Albanese presser

articles.title: Government halves fuel excise to cut price on petrol and diesel

children.content: The government has halved the fuel excise on petrol and diesel for three months, reducing the cost of fuel by 26.3 cents per litre.

items.title: What Australia could learn from the G7's fuel crisis measures

children.content: Australia is not alone in weathering rising fuel costs and issues with domestic supply and distribution amid the war in the Middle East. G7 nations are also working out ways to ease the crisis.

items.title: 'Breaking point': Ex-greyhound racing insider speaks out as inquiry begins

items.title: Secret tunnels used in plot to smuggle one tonne of cocaine into Australia, police say

children.content: Australian police have charged six crew members of the high-profile MV Raider vessel with drug smuggling, saying they attempted to bring 1 tonne of cocaine into Australia after being intercepted by French Polynesian authorities earlier this year.

items.title: Five easy seafood dinners for busy weeknights
